body:
# Bug report

## Current behavior

```tsx
      <Menu.Trigger
        className={styles.Button}
        onClick={() => {
          console.log("clicked trigger");
        }}
      >
        Song <ChevronDownIcon className={styles.ButtonIcon} />
      </Menu.Trigger>
```

`onClick` is never called.

## Expected behavior

`onClick` is called.

## Reproducible example

https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/vigorous-tree-nl9y5q?file=%2Fsrc%2FApp.tsx%3A17%2C46

## Base UI version

1.0.0-alpha.8

## Which browser are you using?

Chrome

## Which OS are you using?

Mac OS

## Which assistive tech are you using (if applicable)?

Voiceover/JAWS etc.

## Additional context

Provide any additional context that might help us identify the problem and find a solution.

comments.body:
The internal backdrop covers the trigger after `mousedown` and so `click` (or `mouseup`) can never fire with the pointer. Keyboard usage does work though.

- `modal={false}` will prevent this
- `onOpenChange` will work

What's the use case for knowing if the trigger was clicked this way?

`onOpenChange` is a good workaround, it was just surprising to me that TypeScript reported `onClick` as a valid prop but at runtime it did nothing.

This might be important when composing with existing components that have their own click handlers.

I wonder if the backdrop cutout prop that was added for Menubar should be added for all triggers when the popup is `modal` @michaldudak. This means the backdrop won't cover the trigger and block events (I think modal Dialog shouldn't have this, though? Although since that isn't triggered on `mousedown`, it shouldn't affect it to begin with)
Yeah, we can do it. This will also enable hover styles on a menu trigger while the menu is open.

This fix will be available in the next npm release of Base UI.

In the meantime, you can try it out on our Canary release channel:

```sh
npm i https://pkg.pr.new/@base-ui-components/react@2141
```
Calling `onClick` on `Trigger` still does nothing (1.0.0-beta.1). How else could I trigger an action And close the modal? `onMouseDown` does work, but why not `onClick`?

@piscopancer you can see with the original Sandbox posted by @OliverJAsh that it _does_ now call `onClick`. The internal backdrop which previously blocked the mouseup is now cutout so that the mouseup fires `onClick` correctly. Do you have a reproduction where that isn't happening?

Though I think using `onOpenChange` is better anyway because it fires at the correct time (on mousedown) while also supporting keyboard clicks.

labels.name: component: menu
labels.description: Changes related to the menu component.

labels.name: has workaround
labels.description: There’s a bug, but users have a complete workaround, so no urgent fix or release is needed.

title: [menu] Trigger `onClick` is not called
